MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HOWELL MOUNTAIN VINTERS & GROWERS ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Howell Mountain Vinters & Growers Association's demurrer to plaintiff Sulaiman Akrami first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed.

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT C. MONDAVI & FAMILY DBA CHARLES KRUG WINDERY MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant C. Mondavi & Family dba Charles Krug Winer's motion to strike the prayers for exemplary damages and attorney's fees in the first amended complaint is granted. In order to state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, a complaint must set forth the elements stated in the general punitive damage statute, Civil Code section 3294, including allegations that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud or malice. Civ. Code 3294(a). "Malice" is defined as conduct "intended by the defendant to cause injury to plaintiff, or despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others." Civ. Code 3294(c)(1). The mere carelessness or ignorance of the defendant does not justify the imposition of punitive damages. Further, plaintiff has failed to allege any statutory or contractual basis for an award of attorney's fees.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Caridad Pena-colon, Esq. 916-638-6610 of the Law offices of John A. Biard for Defendant C. MONDAVI & FAMILY DBA CHARLES KRUG WINDERY through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT C. MONDAVI & FAMILY DBA CHARLES KRUG WINDERY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant C. Mondavi & Family dba Charles Krug Winery's demurrer to the first cause of action for general negligence in plaintiff's first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to allege facts showing a special relationship that would support a duty on the part of defendant C. Mondavi & Family to protect plaintiff from harmful conduct of third parties or that any breach of such duty was a substantial cause of plaintiff's injuries. (See Wise v. Superior Court (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1008, 1013 ["A complaint which lacks facts to show that a duty of care was owed is fatally defective . . . . In general, one owes no duty to control the conduct of a third person to prevent him from causing physical harm to another, absent a special relationship between the defendant and either the person whose conduct needs to be controlled or the foreseeable victim of that conduct."].) Further, plaintiff has not alleged any basis to overcome the statutory immunity set forth in Business & Professions Code section 25602 ["No person who sells, furnishes, gives, or causes to be sold, furnished, or given away, any alcoholic beverage pursuant to subdivision (a) of this section shall be civilly liable to any injured person or the estate of such person for injuries inflicted on that person as a result of intoxication by the consumer of such alcoholic beverage"]; see Ennabe v. Manosa (2014) 58 Cal.4th 697, 722 ("Where injuries are proximately caused by excess alcohol consumption, our Legislature has carefully balanced the interests involved and settled on a rule generally precluding liability for those who provide alcoholic beverages, on the ground that 'the consumption of alcoholic beverages rather than the serving of alcoholic beverages [is] the proximate cause of injuries inflicted upon another by an intoxicated person.'"]; Leong v. San Francisco Parking, Inc. (1991) 235 Cal.App.3d 827, 831-34.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

RULING - A R G U E D.The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PERNOD RICARD USA's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Pernod Ricard USA's demurrer to the first and second cause of action for general negligence and premises liability in plaintiff Sulaiman Akrami's first amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Mr. Akrami has failed to allege facts showing a special relationship that would support a duty on the part of defendant Pernord Ricard USA to protect Mr. Akrami from harmful conduct of third parties and that any breach of such duty was a substantial cause of Mr. Akrami's injuries. (See Wise v. Superior Court (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1008, 1013, ["A complaint which lacks facts to show that a duty of care was owed is fatally defective . . . In general, one owes no duty to control the conduct of a third person to prevent him from causing physical harm to another, absent a special relationship between the defendant and either the person whose conduct needs to be controlled or the foreseeable victim of that conduct. "]). Mr. Akkami is given leave to allege such facts, if he can do so in good faith, in light of California's liberal policy favoring amendments to complaints.

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
